title: The perfect spring weekend starts with daffodils
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Visit daffodil fields in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and Connecticut for a spring weekend activity.
Zach Bryan plans to transform a Lowell church into a Jack Kerouac Center with a museum and performance space.
JetBlue increases checked baggage fees by $4 during off-peak and $9 during peak travel periods for economy passengers.

title: Matt Damon and Noah Kahan will join forces on ‘SNL’ in May
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Matt Damon will host 'Saturday Night Live' on May 9, 2026, with Noah Kahan as the musical guest.
Kahan will appear on 'SNL' for the second time shortly after releasing his new album 'The Great Divide' on April 25.
Damon, a Cambridge native, is hosting 'SNL' for the third time; his first hosting was in 2002.

title: Land court backs Somerville church’s plan for homeless shelter that raised neighborhood concerns
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A Land Court judge ruled that First Church Somerville can build a 26-bed homeless shelter, citing the Dover Amendment.
The shelter's 'low-barrier' status raised neighborhood safety concerns, but the church plans to open soon with proper communication.
Somerville Homeless Coalition will run the new shelter, replacing a cramped 16-bed facility at 64 College Ave.

title: Everett moves to rein in data centers as pushback grows across Massachusetts
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Everett's Planning Board endorses zoning ordinance to restrict data centers in the Docklands Innovation District.
Proposed ordinance would require special permits, limit size to 20,000 square feet or 5 megawatts in EDID.
Statewide, 45 data centers exist; resistance grows despite Gov. Healey's support for more centers.

title: South Shore med spa owner pleads guilty to administering thousands of fake Botox and other treatments
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli pleaded guilty to charges related to administering fake cosmetic injections in Boston federal court.
Fadanelli imported counterfeit Botox and other products from China and Brazil, conducting over 2,700 procedures.
Clients paid Fadanelli more than $1 million for procedures she was not licensed to perform; sentencing is July 1.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about praise from Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla for his leadership amid the team's roster changes.
Mazzulla credited the Celtics' success to player contributions, highlighting Jaylen Brown's improved performance.
Celtics President Brad Stevens commended Mazzulla's coaching staff for maintaining team chemistry amid challenges.

title: ‘That tells me a lot’: Alex Cora impressed by Trevor Story’s response to early-season adversity
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Trevor Story's recent performance, including a two-run double, helped the Red Sox win 3-2 against the Brewers.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora praised Story's determination to stay in the lineup despite early-season struggles.
Story previously improved his performance mid-season in 2025, boosting his OPS post-Milwaukee series last year.

title: Swansea police recover 5 guns from home after report that second grader brought weapon to school
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Swansea police recovered five unregistered guns and ammunition from a home after a report of a second grader bringing a gun to school.
No weapons were found at Mark G. Hoyle Elementary School after a K-9 sweep ensured the campus was safe.
Charges will be filed against two residents in connection with the unsecured guns; neither had a valid Firearms Identification Card.

title: He grew up in R.I., named himself after Ted Williams, and is now drawing comparisons to Elvis and Roy Orbison. Meet Tyler Ballgame.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Tyler Ballgame, a Rhode Island native and Berklee alumnus, is gaining attention for his unique music style blending genres.
Ballgame's debut album "For the First Time, Again" is creating buzz with comparisons to artists like Elvis and Roy Orbison.
Once named Tyler Perry, he reinvented himself in Los Angeles and now tours internationally, including upcoming shows in Boston.

title: For $225k, you could live in America’s oldest shopping mall
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The Arcade in Providence, built in 1828, is America's oldest enclosed shopping mall, now housing 48 residences.
Unit 314 in The Arcade is a 265-square-foot microloft listed for $225,000, located on the third floor.
The unit features a kitchenette, bathroom with standing shower, and a bedroom with office space, suitable for short-term stays.
